div1.entryFree: Receptacle (réceptacle), a place destined to receive what life secerns from itself; used of graves and sewers: O sacred r. of my joys, Tit. I, 92. this fell devouring r. II, 3, 235. in a vault, an ancient r. Rom. IV, 3, 39. empty old --s, or common shores, of filth, Per. IV, 6, 186.
